questions.question:
Which of the following functionality must be implemented by a transport protocol over and above the network protocol?



GATE CSE 2003 | Question: 28

Choose the best matching between \( \text{Group 1} \) and \( \text{Group 2} \)

\[ \begin{array}{|l|l|}\hline \textbf{Group-1} & \textbf{Group-2} \\\hline  \text{P. Data link layer} & \text{1. Ensures reliable transport of data over a physical point-}\\ & \text{to-point link} \\\hline   \text{Q. Network layer} & \text{2. Encodes/decodes data for physical transmission} \\\hline  \text{R. Transport layer} & \text{3. Allows end-to-end communication between two }\\ & \text{processes} \\\hline \text{} & \text{4. Routes data from one network node to the next} \\\hline \end{array} \]



GATE CSE 2004 | Question: 15

Match the following:
\[ \begin{array}{|ll|ll|} \hline \text{(P)} & \text{SMTP} &(1)& \text{Application layer} \\\hline \text{(Q)} & \text{BGP}& (2) & \text{Transport layer} \\\hline   \text{(R)} & \text{TCP}& (3) & \text{Data link layer} \\\hline  \text{(S)} & \text{PPP}& (4) & \text{Network layer} \\\hline &&(5) & \text{Physical layer}  \\\hline \end{array} \]



GATE CSE 2007 | Question: 70

Assume that source S and destination D are connected through two intermediate routers labeled R. Determine how many times each packet has to visit the network layer and the data link layer during a transmission from S to D.



GATE CSE 2013 | Question: 14
options: Network layer – \( 4 \) times and Data link layer – \( 4 \) times
Network layer – \( 4 \) times and Data link layer – \( 3 \) times
Network layer – \( 4 \) times and Data link layer – \( 6 \) times
Network layer – \( 2 \) times and Data link layer – \( 6 \) times
questions.correct_answer: Network layer – \( 4 \) times and Data link layer – \( 6 \) times

options: Medium Access Control sub-layer and Channel sharing

Match the following:
\[ \begin{array}{|l|l|}\hline \textbf{Field} & \textbf{Length in bits} \\\hline  \text{P. UDP Header's Port Number} & \text{I.   48} \\ \text{Q. Ethernet MAC Address} & \text{II.  8} \\  \text{R. IPv6 Next Header} & \text{III. 32}  \\  \text{S. TCP Header's Sequence Number} & \text{IV. 16}  \\\hline \end{array} \]



GATE CSE 2018 | Question: 13
